qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-side template injection in entrypoint redirect middleware leaks request data via Go template evaluation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a TLS ClientAuth Bypass vulnerability in HTTP/3 (QUIC) connections. This finding is about server-side template injection in the HTTP entrypoint redirect middleware via Go text/template evaluation. The vulnerable code (middlewares/redirect/redirect.go), attack vector (template injection via request path), and security consequence (request header leakage) are completely different from the advisory's HTTP/3 TLS authentication bypass.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated API endpoints expose full routing configuration including internal backend URLs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a TLS ClientAuth Bypass vulnerability in HTTP/3 (QUIC) connections. This finding is about unauthenticated access to the API endpoint exposing routing configuration. The vulnerable code (api/handler.go), attack vector (unauthenticated GET request to /api), and security consequence (information disclosure of backend URLs) are fundamentally different from the advisory's HTTP/3 TLS authentication bypass which would allow bypassing client certificate authentication requirements.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a TLS ClientAuth Bypass on HTTP/3 (CVE-2025-68121) in Traefik, which would allow attackers to bypass TLS client certificate authentication requirements when connecting over HTTP/3/QUIC. The two qualifying findings discovered are unrelated vulnerabilities: (1) server-side template injection in HTTP redirect middleware via Go template evaluation in the request path, and (2) unauthenticated API configuration disclosure exposing backend URLs. Neither finding involves HTTP/3, QUIC protocol, TLS client certificate authentication, or authentication bypass mechanisms. The findings target different code paths, use different attack vectors, and result in different security impacts.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2025-68121 describes TLS ClientAuth Bypass on HTTP/3 connections
Advisory affects Traefik v2.11.36 and earlier, v3.6.7 and earlier
Finding 1 targets middlewares/redirect/redirect.go for template injection in HTTP entrypoint redirect
Finding 2 targets api/handler.go for unauthenticated API access
Neither finding references HTTP/3, QUIC, or TLS client authentication mechanisms
Neither finding demonstrates bypassing client certificate authentication requirements
